goal: Remediate the 2026-07-02 code-first repo review of coderef-core across three tiers. Phase 1 (P0 correctness and trust): fix coderef-query missing analyze() call; gate the hollow breaking-change detector; make RAG incremental indexing state record only successful embeds and wire chunksToDelete to vectorStore.delete(); add .coderef/** to scanner DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS and remove committed residue; replace hardcoded machine paths in coderef-watch and coderef-rag-server; fix the two failing vitest tests for a green baseline. Phase 2 (P1 structural, deferred): legacy analyzer/query stack retirement per DR-PHASE-5-C, tree-sitter TS relationship extraction, strict tsconfig migration, shared provider/store factory, coderef-search fix-or-drop, centralized path normalization. Phase 3 (P2 perf and hygiene, deferred): single-parse scan pipeline, dead-code deletion, plugin system wiring, sqlite-vec or atomic vector-store writes.

tasks.description: Load Phase 1 inputs: stub context coderef/working/repo-review-2026-07-remediation/context.md (P0-1..P0-6), report coderef/reference/repo-review-2026-07-02.md section 6, and this WO's analysis.json risks.

Baseline: run npx vitest run; confirm exactly the 2 known failures (buildDependencyGraph.test.ts ENOENT mkdir .test-project/.coderef; ast-mode recursive-mode test) and record counts (last known 1660 pass / 2 fail / 30 skip).
P0-1 fix coderef-query end-to-end: invoke analyzer.analyze() (src/cli/coderef-query.ts:86-95) before dispatching query types; verify each --type returns non-empty results on a coderef-core self-scan.

P0-2 gate breaking-changes until real: make coderef-analyze --type=breaking-changes fail loudly with an explicit not-implemented error (or remove the wiring) instead of returning silently-empty results from the placeholder stubs at diff-analyzer.ts:130-160; document in --help.

P0-3 RAG incremental truth: persist indexed-state only for chunks whose embed SUCCEEDED (indexing-orchestrator.ts ~line 919) and wire chunksToDelete through vectorStore.delete() in the incremental path so removed code leaves the vector store.
P0-3 regression tests: a chunk whose embed fails is NOT recorded as indexed; a chunk removed from source triggers vectorStore.delete(). Add to src/integration/rag/__tests__/.
P0-4a stop self-ingestion: add .coderef/** (and the repo-root .coderef-rag-index.json state file) to DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS in src/scanner/scanner.ts and the mirrored default in src/index.ts.

P0-4b remove committed residue: git rm -r --cached the committed rag-vectors.sqlite/ directory residue and any committed .coderef self-scan artifacts; add matching .gitignore entries so they cannot return.

P0-5 remove hardcoded machine paths: replace C:/Users/willh defaults at coderef-watch.ts:233/274 and coderef-rag-server.ts:386 with env-var override + cwd-relative fallback; no literal user-profile paths remain in src/.

P0-6 fix the 2 failing tests: create the .test-project fixture dir (recursive mkdir) in buildDependencyGraph.test.ts setup; fix the ast-mode recursive-mode fixture/expectation so it passes from a clean checkout.
Green baseline gate: npx vitest run completes with 0 failures (skips unchanged).

Pipeline verification: npm run build, then fresh scan + populate + validate on coderef-core itself; confirm ghost .coderef index entries (types.d.ts / scanner.js class) are gone, validation report is clean, and coderef-watch + coderef-rag-server still start on Windows.
Commit Phase 1 on main by EXPLICIT path (never git add -A) with a plain-English message referencing WO-REPO-REVIEW-2026-07-REMEDIATION-001 Phase 1 / STUB-W45PTN.

Hard stop: report Phase 1 results back (test counts, element-count delta from the exclude change, reindex outcome). Operator review gates Phase 2 planning: author Phase 2 tasks against STUB-XG7DSB only after this review, reconciling STUB-9F63EJ overlap first.

phases.name: repo-review-p1-structural
phases.authoring_notes: Authored post-Phase-1 review per rolling_plan.next_phase_authoring (ad-hoc plan.json edit). STUB-9F63EJ reconciled: intelligence-server retirement ALREADY SHIPPED 2026-06-13 (CHANGELOG.md:96, ruling A executed; docs/intelligence-api.md tombstoned) — no overlap with P1-7; only residue is a stale package-lock.json bin entry, folded into P2-T8. Operator rulings 2026-07-02: coderef-query KEPT, reimplemented on canonical graph.json (preserves ASSISTANT doc-discovery consumer); coderef-search DELETED.
phases.description: P1 structural tier: legacy analyzer/query stack retirement per DR-PHASE-5-C (port coderef-analyze + reimplement coderef-query on canonical .coderef/graph.json, then delete the legacy in-memory graph stack), scanner TS relationship extraction via tree-sitter facts, tsconfig hardening (noEmitOnError + strict for src/pipeline), shared LLM-provider/vector-store factory, coderef-search deletion, centralized path normalization with entity-registry UUID fix.

tasks.description: Load Phase 2 inputs: stub context coderef/working/repo-review-2026-07-remediation/context.md (P1-7..P1-12), report coderef/reference/repo-review-2026-07-02.md section 6 P1 table, Phase 1 results (commit 981173b), and the two operator rulings (keep coderef-query on canonical graph; drop coderef-search).

Pre-phase baseline: npx vitest run must be green (expected 1665 pass / 30 skip / 0 fail per Phase 1 close). Record counts; any failure here is pre-existing drift to triage BEFORE structural work starts.

P1-7a: create a canonical-graph query module (src/query/canonical-graph.ts): load .coderef/graph.json (ExportedGraph) + .coderef/index.json, resolve a friendly --target (file path suffix or element id) to canonical node ids, and provide direction-correct helpers over resolutionStatus='resolved' edges: callersOf/calleesOf (call edges), importersOf/importsOf (import edges), dependentsOf/dependenciesOf (call+import union, transitive with maxDepth), shortestPath/allPaths. Friendly error when graph.json is missing (point at populate).

P1-7b: reimplement coderef-query CLI on canonical-graph.ts keeping the 8 query types and flag surface. Direction semantics anchored on natural reading + the live ASSISTANT doc-discovery consumer: '-me' suffixed types are INBOUND (what-calls-me/what-imports-me/what-depends-on-me = who calls/imports/depends on the target); bare types are OUTBOUND (what the target calls/imports/depends on). Update help text to match implementation exactly. --patterns becomes accepted-but-ignored with a deprecation warning (no more in-memory analysis).

P1-7c: port the 5 AnalyzerService-dependent coderef-analyze types onto canonical artifacts: graph (print canonical graph summary), impact (transitive inbound BFS via canonical-graph.ts), multi-hop (usedBy/calls/dependsOn via canonical-graph.ts), complexity + middleware (elements from .coderef/index.json instead of convertGraphToElements(legacy graph)). The 6 standalone types (config/contract/db/dependency/pattern/docs) and the breaking-changes gate are untouched.

P1-7d: delete the legacy stack — src/analyzer/graph-builder.ts, src/analyzer/graph-analyzer.ts, src/analyzer/analyzer-service.ts, src/query/query-executor.ts, src/context/multi-hop-traversal.ts — and rewire consumers: remove legacy exports from src/analyzer/index.ts + src/context/index.ts + src/index.ts:22 (export * from query-executor); rewire src/context/context-generator.ts and src/context/breaking-change-detector/index.ts off AnalyzerService (gate or port — BCD is already hard-gated). Then grep-verify and delete now-orphaned files ONLY if zero importers remain (candidates: src/analyzer/graph-error.ts, graph-helpers.ts, import-parser.ts, call-detector.ts, src/adapter/graph-to-elements.ts, src/context/impact-simulator.ts — impact-simulator ports to canonical if coderef-analyze impact still needs it). Update any test files that imported deleted modules. NOTE: this removes symbols from the package public API — sanctioned by DR-PHASE-5-C + operator ruling A.

P1-7e: direction-semantics regression tests for the new coderef-query engine (src/query/__tests__/canonical-graph.test.ts): fixture graph with known call/import edges asserting inbound vs outbound for all 6 relationship types plus a path query — the class of bug that motivated the retirement (inverted what-calls-me). Plus a coderef-analyze self-scan smoke (graph/impact/multi-hop non-empty on coderef-core's own .coderef).

P1-11 (operator ruling: drop): delete src/cli/coderef-search.ts and its package.json bin entry; regenerate package-lock.json (npm install --package-lock-only) which also clears the stale coderef-intelligence-server bin residue at package-lock.json:38 (STUB-9F63EJ reconciliation residue).

P1-8: fix TS relationship extraction in the Scanner path: route ts/tsx relationship extraction through tree-sitter facts (as PipelineOrchestrator does) instead of plain-Acorn JSCallDetector (js-parser.ts:21 silently yields empty calls[]/imports[] on TS syntax); remove or bypass the dead TS-node visitor branches (visitor.ts:159-177). MUST add the missing test: non-empty calls[] AND imports[] for a real .ts fixture through scanCurrentElements — currently zero coverage of this hole. Canonical populate pipeline is unaffected; js path stays on Acorn.

P1-9: type-system hardening: set noEmitOnError:true in tsconfig.json (and tsconfig.cli.json if separate) and fix any errors it surfaces; add per-directory strict migration starting with src/pipeline — a tsconfig.pipeline-strict.json (strict:true, noEmit:true, include src/pipeline) + npm script typecheck:pipeline, driven to zero errors. Remaining directories are follow-up scope (Phase 3 or stub), not this task.

P1-10: extract ONE shared LLM-provider/vector-store factory consuming src/integration/llm/model-registry.ts; refactor the 4 copies (rag-index.ts:269-371, rag-search.ts:264-361, coderef-mcp-server.ts:780-801, rag-eval.ts:153-176) onto it and replace coderef-rag-server.ts:240's hardcoded dim table with MODEL_REGISTRY. HARD CONSTRAINT: Ollama LOCAL-ONLY defaults — the factory must never require OpenAI/Anthropic keys for default operation.

P1-12: centralize path normalization: route the ~60 hand-rolled .replace(/\\/g,'/') sites in non-test src/ through normalizeProjectPath (src/utils/coderef-id.ts) or a thin named wrapper; fix the EntityRegistry UUID identity gap (entity-registry.ts:31-34 keys on UNNORMALIZED file:name:line) so Windows-vs-posix spellings yield ONE identity, and add the first entity-registry normalization test. Windows-first verification required; check semantic-registry churn on self-scan (UUID re-keying risk) and report the delta.
Green gate: npx vitest run completes with 0 failures TWICE consecutively (deterministic, per Phase 1 discipline). Skips may change only by explicitly-justified deltas (deleted legacy tests).

Pipeline + bin verification: npm run build clean under noEmitOnError; fresh scan + populate + validate on coderef-core itself — element counts vs the 2591/276 baseline explained by this phase's deletions only; validation report stays the locked 14 fields; startup-probe coderef-query, coderef-analyze, coderef-watch, coderef-rag-server on Windows; confirm doc-discovery's coderef-query invocation shape still works (--type=what-depends-on-me returns inbound dependents).
Commit Phase 2 on main by EXPLICIT path (never git add -A) with a plain-English message referencing WO-REPO-REVIEW-2026-07-REMEDIATION-001 Phase 2 / STUB-XG7DSB; push and verify origin holds HEAD.

Hard stop: report Phase 2 results back (deletion inventory, API-surface changes, test counts, element/registry deltas, strict-migration state). Operator review gates Phase 3 planning: author Phase 3 tasks against STUB-01DW28 only after this review.

phases.name: repo-review-p2-perf-hygiene
phases.description: P2 perf & hygiene tier (STUB-01DW28): scanner single-read/single-parse + true per-file regex fallback + O(n) comment detection, gated on hard scan-output parity; dead-code deletion (stale pipeline cache copy, dead generator, permanently-disabled Anthropic enricher, no-op flags, src/plugins per ruling, src/errors); vector store honest rename + atomic writes; duplicate edge-id fix; test hygiene (un-skip mocked ollama suite, run the 5 orphaned .test.mjs); shared CLI arg helper adopted across the RAG-family bins.

tasks.description: Load Phase 3 inputs: stub context coderef/working/repo-review-2026-07-remediation/context.md rows P2-13..P2-18, report coderef/reference/repo-review-2026-07-02.md section 6 P2 table, Phase 2 results (commit 4443c6c; baselines 2504 elements / 268 files, suite 1523 pass / 30 skip), the verified inventory (read-only scout 2026-07-02: all six P2 items present; graph.json 30711 edges vs 29763 unique ids = 948 excess duplicates), and the two operator rulings (P2-15: REMOVE src/plugins/; P2-16: honest rename + atomic writes, NO sqlite-vec).

Record pre-change baseline: full vitest suite (expect 1523 pass / 30 skip / 0 fail) and a TIMED self-scan over this repo; capture wall-clock seconds, element count, file count, and a sorted element-identity snapshot (id list) to a scratch file for the P3-T6 parity gate.

Scanner perf 1/3 - make the regex pass a true per-file fallback: in src/scanner/scanner.ts scanCurrentElements (regex section ~:1389; fallbackEnabled default :1128), skip regex processing for files whose tree-sitter parse SUCCEEDED; regex still runs for tree-sitter failures/unsupported languages and when useTreeSitter=false. Keep deduplicateElements for remaining mixed cases.

Scanner perf 2/3 - one read + one parse per file per engine: pass the in-memory content scanner.ts already holds (:1109) into TreeSitterScanner.scanFile (kills the re-read at tree-sitter-scanner.ts:189); restructure JSCallDetector so detectImports+detectCalls share ONE parse (js-call-detector/index.ts:106,150 each currently re-read+re-parse via js-parser.ts:133); stop constructing a fresh JSCallDetector per file (scanner.ts:1198-1201, :1319-1324; scanner-worker.ts:60,79-80,130-131 same pattern).

Scanner perf 3/3 - replace O(lines^2) comment/template detection (scanner.ts:1525-1636: isInsideMultiLineComment/isInsideTemplateString rescan from line 0 for every queried line) with a single-pass precomputed per-line state table; fix the per-match full-file Babel re-parse in frontend-call extraction (locate via grep under fileGeneration/frontend-call path; cache the parsed AST per file).

Perf + parity gate: re-run the timed self-scan; REQUIRE identical element identity set and file count vs the P3-T2 snapshot (output parity is a HARD gate - on ANY delta, HALT and report the diff; do not accept silent scan-output change); record before/after wall-clock. If still >10s, note bounded-concurrency async reads as a follow-up candidate - do not force it into this phase.

Dead-code sweep (all verified zero-importer/no-op): git rm src/pipeline/incremental-cache.ts (stale copy; live one is src/cache/incremental-cache.ts), src/generator/generateFrontendCalls.ts, src/semantic/llm-enricher.ts + its test (constructor self-disables at :59-65 - permanently dead, Anthropic-based, conflicts with ollama-local-only anyway); rewire: src/semantic/orchestrator.ts (drop never-true enrichLLM branches :124-125,:213-214 - behavior-preserving since isAvailable() is always false), src/semantic/index.ts (drop LLMEnricher/enrichMetadata/enrichBatch re-exports :11-12), src/semantic/registry-sync.ts type import :21; remove no-op flags: populate.ts --llm-enrich (:52,:92,:175-176, help :234) and scan.ts --plugins/--no-plugins (:158-166, help :43-44).

Plugin system removal (operator ruling A, 2026-07-02T09:37Z): git rm src/plugins/ entirely (8 files, ~2167 LOC, zero importers outside the directory); clean the two non-code references: src/semantic/header-generator.ts:70 layer-inference pattern entry for src/plugins/ and its test at header-generator.test.ts:339-340.

Retire src/errors/ (six exception classes with ZERO production importers; alive only via legacy root index.ts:24 export + isolated __tests__/errors.test.ts + demo-all-modules.ts): git rm src/errors/ + __tests__/errors.test.ts; rewire root index.ts (drop the export line) and demo-all-modules.ts (:46,:53 CodeRefError usage). Record in communication.json: package API change - CodeRefError/FileNotFoundError/IndexError/ParseError/ScanError/ValidationError no longer exported from the legacy main entry.

Vector store honesty + crash safety (operator ruling A): rename the misleading sqlite store to json (src/integration/vector/sqlite-store.ts -> json-store.ts, class renamed to match); KEEP the string "sqlite" accepted as a DEPRECATED alias in provider-factory createVectorStore + CLI --store values (warn once, map to json store) so no existing caller breaks; make save() atomic via temp-file+rename (currently direct writeFileSync at :237-248); add tests covering atomic write + alias mapping. The O(n) cosine scan stays (documented limitation, not this phase).

Fix duplicate edge ids in the canonical graph (948 excess entries across 456 duplicated ids live): add seen-id dedupe at edge emit in src/pipeline/graph-builder.ts buildEdges (:393; computeEdgeId at :750-762 hashes source:relationship:target:file:line and identical tuples are pushed by multiple passes today); add a regression test asserting emitted graph edge ids are unique; run the pipeline invariant suite - if a frozen-fixture edge-count expectation shifts SOLELY due to dedupe, update it with documented evidence (an honest artifact cannot carry duplicate ids); locked index.json/validation schemas MUST keep passing.

Test hygiene (P2-17): un-skip the fully-mocked "Ollama Provider - Unreachable Daemon" suite (src/integration/llm/__tests__/ollama-provider-unreachable.test.ts:22); the :134 "Runtime Negative Test (Manual)" block STAYS skipped with a comment saying why; add **/*.test.mjs to vitest include and make the 5 orphaned mjs suites run green (fix or convert - they have never executed); drop the dead processingTimeMs determinism-mask field in the indexing-gate test (:73). chroma/pinecone describe.skips stay (need external services).

Create ONE shared CLI arg/exit helper src/cli/shared/cli-args.ts: supports --flag=value AND --flag value for every value-taking flag, NaN-checked numeric parsing (reject with message + exit 1), consistent unknown-flag error + exit-code contract; full coderef header; unit test alongside.

Adopt the shared helper across the copy-pasted RAG-family parsers: rag-search.ts (:58-171 - fixes --top-k=/--min-score=/--lang=/--type=/--file= silently swallowing the NEXT arg), rag-index.ts (:59-95 same copy-pasted bug), rag-eval.ts (:112), rag-status.ts (:50); NaN-checked parseInt everywhere (rag-search.ts:145,188); document the scan.ts positional-first requirement in its help text (:148). Remaining hand-rolled parsers (populate, coderef-watch, coderef-pipeline, coderef-rag-server, scan-frontend-calls, validate-routes) are noted as follow-up, NOT forced into this phase.

Residue + docs sweep: delete untracked residue graphify-out/ (4.3MB html+json, dated 2026-05-12) and mcp-smoke-stderr.txt (both confirmed unreferenced by any code/config); docs: CHANGELOG.md Phase 3 entry (Removed/Changed/Fixed), docs/CLI.md (--plugins/--no-plugins/--llm-enrich gone; --store json canonical with sqlite as deprecated alias; rag-search --flag=value fixed), README.md wherever it mentions plugins/errors exports.

Full suite green x2 consecutively; reconcile counts vs the 1523 pass / 30 skip baseline with an EXACT delta explanation (+5 unskipped ollama-mock tests, +N from the 5 mjs suites, +new tests from T10/T11/T13, -errors.test.ts, -llm-enricher.test.ts, +/- header-generator pattern test tweaks). Any unexplained delta = HALT.

End-to-end verification on Windows: npm run build (reconcile dist - partial-emit lesson from Phase 2), timed self-scan re-baseline (record new element/file counts + wall-clock; reconcile any element delta exactly against the deletions), locked 14-field validation report present + PASS, typecheck:pipeline 0 errors, populate headers 0 stale, probe coderef-query (all 8 types) and rag-search with --store json AND the sqlite alias warn path.

Commit on main by EXPLICIT path via node SKILLS/GIT/git-commit/run.mjs "msg" --repo=... --done --paths <files> (flags BEFORE --paths; OMIT git-rm-ed files from --paths - staged deletions ride the commit); verify origin. Then ASSISTANT repo: flip STUB-01DW28 deferred_phase -> promoted in TRACKING/stubs.json, commit + push that.

FINAL PHASE. Run the canonical writer (--phase=3 --status=complete --commit=<hash> --verdict=PASS), rewrite terminal-post-exec.txt + HANDOFF-repo-review-2026-07-remediation-001.json (terminal_state waiting_user; next action /close-workorder), report Phase 3 results back to the operator. rolling_plan.close_after_phase=3: the WO is now CLOSE-ELIGIBLE - the operator (not this executor) runs /close-workorder WO-REPO-REVIEW-2026-07-REMEDIATION-001.

phases.authoring_notes: Authored post-Phase-2 review per rolling_plan.next_phase_authoring (ad-hoc plan.json edit). Operator rulings 2026-07-02T09:37Z: P2-15 plugins REMOVE (ruling A); P2-16 vector store honest-rename + atomic writes (ruling A, no sqlite-vec). Inventory re-verified against the post-Phase-2 tree by a read-only scout before authoring (all six P2 items present; 948 excess duplicate edge ids confirmed in live graph.json). Scope also folds in the two items Phase 2 flagged for Phase 3 (duplicate edge-id fix, untracked residue cleanup) and the src/errors retirement (zero production importers after DR-PHASE-5-C).

hard_stops.approved_by: operator directive 'phase 2 please' after Phase 1 report-back (review gate satisfied)
hard_stops.reason: Phase 1 ends with mandatory user review (rolling plan: report back before Phase 2 is planned).
Phase 2 ends with mandatory user review (rolling plan: report back before Phase 3 is planned).
hard_stops.approved_by: operator directive 'review for completion... continue to next phase' after Phase 2 report-back (review gate satisfied; completion re-verified against origin)

hard_stops.reason: Phase 3 is the FINAL phase: terminal hard stop. After the P3-T19 report-back the WO is close-eligible; /close-workorder requires operator approval (rolling_plan.close_after_phase=3).

plan_type_rationale: Forced to phased by the --phased-stubs rolling-plan contract (a strict rolling plan is incoherent per ROLLING-PLAN.md). Stub category 'fix' maps to strict and the user passed --plan-type=strict; both are logged as overridden. Rolling shape chosen because Phase 1 (P0) evidence (green baseline, ghost-entry cleanup, STUB-9F63EJ overlap) changes the picture for the P1 structural tier, so one-shot planning of all 18 items would go stale by Phase 2.

rolling_plan.next_phase_authoring: ad-hoc plan.json edit until /plan-next-phase skill exists (STUB-358)
